TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Seediq, story: sdqNr-childhood_micang 2020s

S.FORM: a yaku a mpprengo ku saya so nodaan mu a.
S.TRANSL: 今天呢，我要來說說我的經歷。
Now, I would like to talk about my experience.

S.FORM: so sbiciq cbeyo a a kndalax ku so nakahara yonense ku a meyah kndalax nakahara meyah ku alang tongan hini.
S.TRANSL: 過去，我小時候呢，四年級時就從中原部落來到眉溪部落這裡。
In the past, as a child, I had come from Nakahara-- I was in the fourth grade then-- and came to Tongan here.

S.FORM: a mesa ka tamabubu mu a uN nita tongan da gaga hii kana ka so dheran ta ma a iya ta eniq anu ka naka nakahara hini de uka a uka phumaan bunga mesa to qahur mesa to btunux ka nakahara mesa ma.
S.TRANSL: 我的父母就說："我們到眉溪吧！我們的土地都在那邊！我們別留在中原這裡了！中原這裡沒有地方種植地瓜，因為土地很貧瘠。"
So my parents said: "Let's go to Tongan! All our land is over there! Let's not stay in Nakahara here. There's no place for us to plant sweet potatoes, because the land is infertile."

S.FORM: uN anu ka kmeepah dheran nami di.
S.TRANSL: 然後我們在我們自己的土地上工作了。
Then we worked on our own land.

S.FORM: kika mesa ka bubu mu a a micang sa as a ta mhuma bunga ho sa a dm a ##sbsawan da ha ta mhuma bunga si ma.
S.TRANSL: 然後，我媽媽說："Micang, 我們去種地瓜吧！" 天氣已經較溫和了，我們來種地瓜吧，她說。
Then, my mother said: "Micang, let's go plant some sweet potatoes!" The weather's cooler now, and let's go plant sweet potatoes, she sai."

S.FORM: ini ku kela mhuma bunga mapa ku bruru ma.
S.TRANSL: 我不會種番薯，而我用背扛著背簍。
I didn't know how to plant sweet potatoes, and I was carrying a basket on my back.

S.FORM: asi mu osai rmemux daha ka bung a waso ma rima ka waso mnenaq bruru di ma.
S.TRANSL: 我馬上迅速地塞了幾根籃子裡的蕃薯藤(到土裡)。
I immediately stick (into the dirt) a couple of sweet potato rattans which were in the basket.

S.FORM: mhedu ku di bubu kesaun mu.
S.TRANSL: 我說，「媽媽!我種完啦！」
"Mom, I have finished planting!" I said.

S.FORM: mosa kus mosa meyah mita bubu mu.
S.TRANSL: 接著媽媽來看
Then my mother came to see.

S.FORM: ga inu ma su wada qheduun rmeus kana ka wahin bunga nii di si ma.
S.TRANSL: 她說：「什麼？你怎麼把所有的地瓜枝葉全給掩埋啦？」
"What? How come you burried all these sweet potato rattans?", she said.

S.FORM: ma teru ba pnhumaan su gheti wa nodaan kingan bruru biciq nii mesa ka bubu mu ma.
S.TRANSL: 她說著：「妳怎麼只有種三(棵)呀？這個小背簍(裡的番薯藤)妳是可以種很大一片地的！」
"Only three? You can actually plant a broad area with (the rattans in) this one basket!", my mother said.

S.FORM: usa da usa sapah di ini su kela ini su kela mhuma bunga.
S.TRANSL: 「走! 回家吧! 妳不會種番薯啦!」
"Go! Go home now! You don't know how to plant sweet potatoes!"

S.FORM: meyah ku sap meyah ku sapah di ma.
S.TRANSL: 我就回家了。
So I came home.

S.FORM: musa ku pure ido wa di ma pure ido rumu wa ido macu.
S.TRANSL: 我就去煮飯了，我煮稀飯，小米粥。
So I went rice cooking, and I cooked porridge, millet prridge.

S.FORM: ma pure ku di ma.
S.TRANSL: 我就煮啦。
So I cooked.

S.FORM: inu dung sqerul dung ido m ido mu macu ma.
S.TRANSL: 可怎麼知道我又燒焦了我的小米。
But, I don't know why, again my millet was burned!

S.FORM: eyahun ku na mseyang dung bubu mu.
S.TRANSL: 我媽媽又回來對我生氣。
Again, my mother came to me angry.

S.FORM: maanu sqerul na ido mac ido nii dungan huwa kesaun mekan ka sqerul si bubu mu ma.
S.TRANSL: 我媽媽說：「妳怎麼又把飯燒焦? 已經煮到這樣焦了的稀飯，怎麼吃啊?」
"Why did you burn the rice again? How do you eat the singe like this?", my mother said.

S.FORM: ma uxe ku pure dhenu wa ini ku kela pure da kesaun mu hi ma.
S.TRANSL: 結果我就不煮了。於是我就對她說：「 我不會煮飯!」
So I didn't cook, as a result. "I don't know how to cook!" I said to her.

S.FORM: ma usa mangan a qucun da meyah pure sa bubu mu ma.
S.TRANSL: 我媽媽說了：「去採陸喬！」，她就過來煮。
Coming here to cook, my mother said "Go take some shallots!".

S.FORM: mhada ka ido dheya di ma musa miyan mangan qucun di ma bunga tumun di ma bunga tumun di ma damac nami ido ma ma anu ka a sari.
S.TRANSL: 煮的飯熟了之後，我們就去採陸喬、圓番薯、南瓜還有芋頭來配飯吃。
The rice was already cooked, so we went out to pluck shallots, round sweet potatoes, pumpkins, and taros as side dishes.

S.FORM: a han na maribu a mangan na ludux bubu mu ma ludux bubu mu ma bobo ido hii ka.
S.TRANSL: 我媽媽先採(所採我們剛採的作物的)嫩葉，放在飯的上面。
First, my mother would take the sprouts (of those we just plucked) and put them on the top of the rice.

S.FORM: anu ka tarux kiya di ma gimaxun na ido hii ka tarux bubu mu ma.
S.TRANSL: 然後，我媽媽就把芋頭莖攪拌到飯裡。
Then, my mother would mix some sweet potato stem into that rice.

S.FORM: ma paru ku hari de wada ku mkela meepah di ma.
S.TRANSL: 然後，等我長大了一點，我就能工作了。
Then, as I grew a little older, I was able to work.

S.FORM: ni ta wada ku gonense da rokunen.
S.TRANSL: 當時我大約五、六年級。
I was in about fifth or sixth grade then.

S.FORM: ni ta di si bubu mu a pahae ta smkeguy di sa.
S.TRANSL: 我媽媽就說：「來吧！我們來剝麻！」
"Let's go!" my mother said "Let's do ramie peeling, then"

S.FORM: uh pahae ta ma ku tmgesa smkeguy si ma ma t tminun ma mnuqah tgesaun ku na bubu mu ma.
S.TRANSL: 她說：「我來教妳剝麻、編織和捻絲吧！」我媽媽說：「我教妳！」。
"Let me teach you how to do ramie-peeling, weaving, and thread-twisting" she said. "I'll teach you" my mother said .

S.FORM: ma a dmaus ma ini ku kela anu ka dmaus ma.
S.TRANSL: 至於整理經線呢，我不會整理經線。
As for organizing threads, I didn't know how to do it.

S.FORM: bbeun na baga mu dungan ma su ini kela dmaus si.
S.TRANSL: 她又打我的手，說：「你怎麼不會整理經線？」
She hit my hand again, saying "How come you don't know how to organize threads?"

S.FORM: mkela ku ka qnnuqah ma mkela ku tngiya dmaus ini ku kela.
S.TRANSL: 我會捻絲，我會繞麻線。但是，整理經線我就是不會。
I knew how to twist the threads, I knew how to spool the threads. But organizing the threads, I just wasn't able to do it.

S.FORM: gisu hmuwa tninun su nii a a bleqi a hmabik ka habik su ga mridih habik su kesaun ku na.
S.TRANSL: 她對著我說：「妳怎麼織啊？綁腰帶時妳的腰帶一定要綁緊！妳的腰帶歪了!」
"How can you weave like this? You've got to fasten your belt when you wear it! You're belt is tilted!" she said to me.

S.FORM: odaan ku na smebuc papak dung ka bubu mu ma.
S.TRANSL: 我媽媽又來，鞭打我的腳。
My mother came again and whipped on my foot.

S.FORM: lmingis ku mesa ka lubi mu.
S.TRANSL: 然後，聽Lubi說，我就哭了。
So I cried, as Lubi said.

S.FORM: iya bbei bubu huwa mesa m.
S.TRANSL: 「別打她了，媽媽!」
"Don't whip her, mother!"

S.FORM: tminun di ma uxe a pkela tminun a beebuun su si bubu ma.
S.TRANSL: Lubi說：「她已經在(學怎麼)織了，如果妳打她，她就學不會了」
"She's already weaving (learning how to weave), and she won't be able to do it if you whip her", said Lubi.

S.FORM: uN lmngelung ku naq di ma thuwe ku naq tminun di malu ka tnninun mu di ma.
S.TRANSL: 後來我自己想了想、慢慢地織，最後我的織布(技術)就變好了。
So I did a lot of thinking myeslf. Slowly I weaved, and my weaving (skill) became good.

S.FORM: o kiya da si bubu mu mqaras bubu mu ma.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽說：「喔! 可以了!」媽媽很開心。
"Oh! You're good now!", said mother. She was happy.

S.FORM: kika kika mhedu mhedu kiya de jyu jyugosai ku di musa ku yosai paran musa mes yosai paran di ma.
S.TRANSL: 那個(學織布)結束後，我就十五歲了。然後我就去霧社做洋裁的工作。
So after that (my traning for weaving) was finished, I turned fifteen years old. I went to do western dressmaking in Paran.

S.FORM: smalu ka lukus ta lukus ta so a lukus a mukan da.
S.TRANSL: 我去製作衣服，像是原住民的衣服，還有漢人所穿的衣服。
I made clothings, like those of the aborigines, and those of the Chinese people.

S.FORM: matak di ma ini ku kela matak duri ma.
S.TRANSL: 我就做剪(裁)的工作，但我也是不會。
I did cutting, and I didn't know how to do it either.

S.FORM: jyugosai ku ciida ma mkela ku qmita ka a mesa yi piri yi piring kiya kelaun mu kiya ma.
S.TRANSL: 我當時十五歲，然後我會看那個他們所說的一piri/piring的布的單位。那個我知道。
I was fifteen years old at that time, and I knew how to tell the measurement of the cloth, which they call one piri/piring. That I know.

S.FORM: ma matak ku uri de wada ku mkela tikuh duri di ma.
S.TRANSL: 我就做剪裁。我也會了一點。
I did the cutting too. And I got to know a little about it.

S.FORM: slaale ku matak de ini mu ciya a wuhun ka lukus kiya di ma.
S.TRANSL: 當我先剪的時候，那個衣服我沒有加五分 (縫製時的長度)
When I first do the cutting, I didn't add five centimeters to the clothing.

S.FORM: mlukus ku di ini usa di wada wada wada.
S.TRANSL: 我穿了衣服，但卻穿不下。
So I wore it, but it didn't fit me.

S.FORM: wada ini anu ka ciya ka anu ka a anu ka konghun na di peni ma ini usa plukusun di ma.
S.TRANSL: 我沒有加公分，結果穿不下。
I didn't add the centimeters; and I couldn't wear it.

S.FORM: mesa ka uh anu ka tnheyaqan ga hmuwa da n isu nii ma i ga su ini ciya di si.
S.TRANSL: 那些跟我一起(學洋裁)的人就說：「你的(縫製的衣服)是怎麼回事?」 他們說：「你沒有加(公分)啊!」
Those who were with me (also learning western sewing there) said: "What's going on with your work (clothings)?" "You're not adding (the centimeters)" they said.

S.FORM: o mesa ku.
S.TRANSL: 我就說：「喔(我知道了)！」
So I said: "Oh (I see)!"

S.FORM: kika mhedu kiya de mhedu miyan yosai de meyah ku sapah.
S.TRANSL: 然後，那個結束了之後，我們學習洋裁結束後，我就回到了家裡。
Then, when that was finished, when we finished learning western dressmaking, I went home.

S.FORM: ma musa nami a meepah da ma sntuku pila ma mhuma kana kosuy cbeyo kiya ka angalun nami pila.
S.TRANSL: 然後，我們就去工作賺錢。以前(那時)即使是種香茅也是可以賺錢的。
Then, we went to work to make money. In the past (at that time), one can make money even by planting lemongrass.

S.FORM: ma maha ma lqelaq sntuku pila tmabun.
S.TRANSL: 我們去水田工作賺錢。我們在那耕作。
We worked at rice paddies to make money. We plowed.

S.FORM: icinici goen bale uri ka han miyan tmabun kiya ma.
S.TRANSL: 我們在(水田)那邊耕作，一天真的只能賺五塊。
Plowing there (at the rice paddies), we were really making just five dollars a day.

S.FORM: ani so kiya de ini nam ini nami a sgaliqi ka pila kiya han miyan me mege a rudan nami a.
S.TRANSL: 即使像那樣，我們也沒有浪費那賺來的錢。而是把錢給了我們的長者。
Despite that, we didn't waste that money. We gave it to our elderlies.

S.FORM: baruyun daha tikuh so beras ma timu.
S.TRANSL: 他們會去買一些東西，像是米和鹽。
They would buy some things like rice and salt.

S.FORM: han daha maruy ma ma mqaras riyung ka so a b bubu nami ma tama nami ma.
S.TRANSL: 他們去買東西，我父母也非常高興。
They bought things, and our parents were very happy.

S.FORM: kika gonense ku de wada hu wada huqin ka tamabubu mu a tama mu wada huqin.
S.TRANSL: 我五年級的時候，我的父親過世了。
When I was in the fifth grade, my father passed away.

S.FORM: a tado cili ka tama mu wada huqin ka tama mu di ma.
S.TRANSL: 我的父親是Tado Cili。他那時過世了。
My father was Tado Cili. He passed away.

S.FORM: so ku bale gluun di ma meepah ku naq da theyaq ku bubu mu meepah ma.
S.TRANSL: (爸爸過世後)我就很可憐，只有工作。我就跟隨著媽媽工作。
(After he passed away,) I was so pitiful, so I just workd. Together with my mother, I worked.

S.FORM: kika jiyu kndalax ku yosai de meyah ku sapah de jyurokusai ku.
S.TRANSL: 從學習洋裁結束後我就回到家裡，那時我十六歲。
When I finished learning western sewing and came home, I was sixteen years old.

S.FORM: eyahun ku daha smrai meyah snrai ka awi di ma.
S.TRANSL: Awi就來向我求婚。
Awi came, and proposed to me.

S.FORM: maha ku jyurokusai.
S.TRANSL: 我那時大約十六歲。
I was about sixteen years old then.

S.FORM: su jyuichigacu ka kekong nami maha ku maha ku jyurokusai jyunigacu de kekong nami ka awi di ma.
S.TRANSL: 我們結婚的時候是十一月。十二月的時候我大約是十六歲了，那時我就跟Awi結婚了。
It was November when we got married. I was about sixteen years old in December, and I was married to Awi.

S.FORM: ma ma kika jyunanasai ku de tuting ku a laqi mu paru.
S.TRANSL: 我十七歲的時候生了老大。
When I was seventeen years old, I gave birth to my first child.

S.FORM: ma so ba gluun ka nodaan nodaan nami ciida duri di ma.
S.TRANSL: 我們那時候也過得很辛苦。
We were also having a tough time in those days.

S.FORM: ga baro nii ka neepah mu a.
S.TRANSL: 我工作的地方就在上面那裏。
The place where I worked is just up above there.

S.FORM: nosaan nami tmukuy a macu ma.
S.TRANSL: 我們去那播小米的種子。
We went to sow millets.

S.FORM: a wada miyan puqulun ka laqi.
S.TRANSL: 我們餵小孩吃飯。
We fed the child.

S.FORM: ma kika ani maanu phumaun kana ani so ppuqun ma so rudan cbeyo so.
S.TRANSL: 以前的老人家只要可以吃的都種。
People in the past planted everything, as long as its edible.

S.FORM: so a tmurak peni ma kerung ma bunga ma tmurak peni ma kerung ma bunga ma phumaun nami ma.
S.TRANSL: 我們種了像是南瓜、佛手瓜、番薯等等的食物。
We've planted things like pumpkins, chayotes, and sweet potatoes.

S.FORM: ma mesa ka seediq mas ma namu ma su a mdurumuc riyung mhuma kesaun ku daha.
S.TRANSL: 有人就對我說：「妳為什麼這麼勤奮的種作物?」
Some people said to me: "Why are you so diligent in plantting?"

S.FORM: ma uka so nii laqi mdurumuc mhuma a ppuqun mesa ka seediq.
S.TRANSL: 族人們說：「沒有這麼認真種作物的孩子！」
"There are no other young children that are so diligent in plantting," people said.

S.FORM: kana beluh pais phumaun mu ma baro gaga ma ani kana seediq neeyah iyu a musa lmamu beluh nu pais ma.
S.TRANSL: 我連紅豆都種，就在那上面。而且好多來自南山溪部落的族人都去摘(我種的)紅豆。
I even plantted adzuki beans, right up there. And all the people from the Iyu tribe went to pick the red beans (that I planted).

S.FORM: a kana sunguc tnukuy mu ma eyahan daha smebuc pgeeluk smebuc ka seediq anu ka alang iyu hini.
S.TRANSL: 我連樹豆都種。南山部落的族人們也都爭先恐後來採收樹豆。
I even planted tree beans, and the Iyu people rushed here for priority to pick the tree beans.

S.FORM: ma kika mqaras riyung ka baki mu duri a tmukuy ku a macu ma.
S.TRANSL: 因為我種小米，我祖父也非常高興。
My grandfather was very happy too, since I was plowing millet.

S.FORM: ma so bi gluun ma so bi lguun.
S.TRANSL: 我為何如此的可憐? 我為何如此的可憐?
Why am I so pitiful? Why am I so pitiful?

S.FORM: tera naku nii da mesa ku muuyas ku baro ga lmingis ku.
S.TRANSL: 我是這樣的可憐! 我會在上面這樣唱著，而且哭了。
I am so pitiful in this way! I would sing these lyrics up there, and I would cry.

S.FORM: lmingis ku a muuyas ma ma lmngelung ku naq so nodaan na bubu mu dahaka taa bubu mu tama mu ini mu so kelai riyung tama mu uri gonense ku wada huqin ka tama mu.
S.TRANSL: 我哭著唱歌，然後就想起了我父母的經歷。但我對爸爸印象並不深，因為他往生時我才五年級。
I sang, crying, and just thought of what my parents had gone through. But I didn't know much about my father, since I was in the fifth grade when he passed away.

S.FORM: lmigis ku posaun ku daha seno laqi musa seno ma.
S.TRANSL: 我哭泣，因為他們在我還小時就把我嫁出。
I cried, because they made me marry when I was little.

S.FORM: ani so kiya we wada mu dehukun peni a dmanga laqi mu ga.
S.TRANSL: 即使那樣(年輕時就結婚)，我還是有盡到做父母親的責任。
Despite that (being married young), I managed to take good care of my kids.

S.FORM: a mm mpitu laqi mu ma daha wada gedang di ma rima ka wa muudus.
S.TRANSL: 我有七個小孩，兩個往生，活著有五個。
I had seven kids. Two passed away, and five are still alive.

S.FORM: a laqi to rseno uri wa ma kingan ba laqi mqedil laqi mu.
S.TRANSL: 我的孩子全都是男生，只有一個是女生。
All of my kids are boys, except for just one who is a girl.

S.FORM: ma ma wisu ku qrinuc dungan saya ma wada ku s hitiun malix dungan ma wisu ku pqrinuc dungan .
S.TRANSL: 我現在又受苦了，因為我自己一個人留下來。我又在受苦了。
I am suffering again now, because I was left alone again. I am suffering again.

S.FORM: ma ma uka naq barahan na kiya da kiya naq so n nodaan ta muudus bobo dheran.
S.TRANSL: 無奈那就是我們生活在這塊土地上，人生必然的遭遇。
Nevertheless, that's just what we have to undergo, living on this land.

S.FORM: a so so nii so wisu meyah so so tmgesa so oda so nii ka laqi so mbarah nii duri a.
S.TRANSL: 就像現在，新生一帶的孩子又再被教導(我們的傳統文化)。
It's just like now, where kids of the new generation are being taught (about our traditions).

S.FORM: malu riyung ka so nodaan so nii a so ini ba chungii ka so a so gaya ta cbeyo ma.
S.TRANSL: 這樣(小孩學習族人的傳統)非常好，因為這麼一來我們的族律就不會被忘記。
This (kids learning the tribal traditions) is very good, because our tribal law would not be fogotten.

S.FORM: laqi a so ini kela mmaanu nii ka laqi saya.
S.TRANSL: 現在的孩子什麼都不會。
Kids nowadays don't know anything.

S.FORM: a say saya de ini pntena so cbeyo ka s saya da.
S.TRANSL: 現在和以前不一樣了。
Things are different from the past now.

S.FORM: a malu riyung ka so nodaan ta saya meyah sa tngesa so a.
S.TRANSL: 我們今天所做的事非常好，來這裡研究(我們的語言)。
What we did today is very good, coming here to study (our language).

S.FORM: so a anu ka laqi so kndalax so a uh tepaq l laqi nii so meyah so tmgesa so oda so so a l lmutuc so kari seediq wisu daha so a eyahun so a so a tmgesa ita hini.
S.TRANSL: 從台北來的孩子們來研究我們的傳統文化，傳承我們的族語，而且來這裡教導我們。
The kids from Taipei came to study our traditions, to pass down our language, and they are coming to teach us here.

S.FORM: a biyo tara ku bi hiya weewa rudu tuxux nii yo sare o sare.
S.TRANSL: 啊！在哪裡一定要等我啊，鳥巢頭的女孩！年輕的小姐，噢，年輕的小姐。
Ah, you must wait for me there, the girl with the nest-like hair. Young lady, oh young lady.

S.FORM: nrabe nomin nrabe nomin he su mtara he su mtara cceka yayung yayung bnaruy.
S.TRANSL: Rabe Nomin, Rabe Nomin! 我會等妳，我會等妳，就在河流的中段，在yayung Bnaruy。
Rabe Nomin, Rabe Nomin! I will wait for you. I will wait for you, right at the middle part of the river, river Bnarury.

S.FORM: nrabe nomin nrabe nomin he su pdehuk he ku pdehuk mtara hido nii hido runge.
S.TRANSL: Rabe Nomin, Rabe Nomin. 妳會到達嗎? 我會到達嗎? 我在等著太陽升起，等待著太陽雨。
Rabe Nomin, Rabe Nomin! Will you arrive? Will you arrive? I am waiting for the sunrise, for the sunshower.

S.FORM: a na ima yaku wa o nay o nay ima duri wa o nay o nay takun sare wa o nay o nay takun sare wa o nay.
S.TRANSL: 啊！我是誰呢，我又是誰？我是Takun Sare哇，我是Takun Sare！
Ah who am I, who am I again? I am Takun Sare, I am Takun Sare!

S.FORM: o nay ima duri wa o nay o nay awi robo a o nay o nay takun sama wa o nay o nay micang tado wa o nay.
S.TRANSL: 這又是誰呢？這是Awi Rob, 這是Takun Sama, 這是Mican Tado！
Who is this, now? This is Awi Robo, this is Takun Sama, and this is Mican Tado!

S.FORM: ima ka soring wa ima ka soring wa nweewa lahuy nii nweewa lahuy nii.
S.TRANSL: 這位少婦是誰呢？這位少婦是誰？這位老婆婆啊，這位老婆婆。
Who is this young wife? Who is this young wife? This old lady, this old lady.

S.FORM: nyami nii da nyami nii da.
S.TRANSL: 這已經是我們的了，這已經是我們的了。
This is ours now, this is ours now.

S.FORM: a ima ku dungan wa nyaku snhabo bi murux.
S.TRANSL: 我又是誰呢？我是Habo，真正單身的Habo。
Who am I again? I am Habo, who is really single.

S.FORM: a ima ku dungan wa nyami nii ita ho nnisu ta bi nii.
S.TRANSL: 我又是誰呢？這是我們的了，這是你的了。
Who am I again? This is ours now, and this is yours now.

S.FORM: mtluhe ta nkari.
S.TRANSL: 我們習慣我們的語言。
We are accustomed to our language.

S.FORM: seediq ta bi bale.
S.TRANSL: 我們是真正的賽德克族人。
We are the true Seediq people.

S.FORM: unnita bi nii da.
S.TRANSL: 這是我們的了。
This is ours now.

S.FORM: nali saya da ho.
S.TRANSL: 就在今天。
Right on today.

S.FORM: nita bi mneyah nalang ta bi tongan.
S.TRANSL: 我們真的是來自南豐部落。
We are truly from the Tongan tribe.

S.FORM: mhuwe su bale.
S.TRANSL: 真的謝謝你！
Thank you very much.
